goal: Create a divide.py module with a divide(a, b) function that performs division with proper error handling for division by zero, along with corresponding pytest tests.
scope: Create divide.py file containing a divide(a, b) function
Function returns a/b for valid inputs
Function raises ValueError when b equals zero
Create test_divide.py with pytest test cases
Include tests for normal division cases
Include test for division by zero error handling
out_of_scope: Handling of special numeric types (complex numbers, etc.)
Input validation beyond division by zero

acceptance_criteria.check: test -f /Users/George/warden/tasks/t-triple/workdir/divide.py && python3 -c "from divide import divide; print('import success')"
Does divide(a, b) correctly return a/b for normal division cases (e.g., divide(10, 2) equals 5)?
Does divide(a, 0) raise ValueError with appropriate error handling?
